stories: Trade-policy shock goes live: Treasury’s Bessent says a global 15% tariff starts this week; “normalization” in ~5 months is the new base-case path
Geopolitics escalates: WSJ says U.S.-Israel strikes expand the Iran conflict; shipping lanes and Gulf logistics stay the market’s focal point
Oil desk reprices higher: Goldman lifts Q2 Brent to $76 (WTI $71) on Hormuz disruption assumptions
Central banks’ “look-through” test: CNBC flags easing-path risk if crude jumps $20–30/bbl (headline CPI impact doubles/triples)
Asia stress test: South Korea’s benchmark plunges double-digits as energy-import shock reverberates across regional equities
China “Two Sessions” watch: stimulus details, deficit targets (~4% chatter), and consumer/property support are the global cyclical swing factor
Earnings tape-driver: Broadcom beats Q1, guides Q2 higher, and authorizes a new $10B buyback
Hormuz insurance is now a tradable variable: Marsh/Aon in talks with the U.S. on insuring tankers transiting Hormuz; implementation could take weeks
Microcap “risk-on pocket” despite macro risk-off: $MOBX +600% day; $EDSA ~+300% on the week (confirmed by CNBC)
Gold snaps back: up >1% as safe-haven demand returns on war + inflation uncertainty
